title: Mildly Fermented Scottish Chickpeas Cornish Pasty
description: A nostalgic British dish featuring fermented chickpeas in a traditional Cornish pasty, perfect for a hearty lunch or snack, this sugar-free-friendly recipe is a unique twist on a classic.
instructions: Rinse the chickpeas and soak them in water for at least 8 hours.
Drain and rinse the chickpeas, then transfer them to a saucepan with 4 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 45 minutes, or until the chickpeas are tender.
In a separate pan, saute the onion, potato, and swede in butter until they are tender.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked chickpeas, sauteed vegetables, and salt, and mix well.
To make the pastry, combine the flour and a pinch of salt in a bowl, then add the butter and mix until the dough comes together.
Roll out the pastry and use it to line a baking dish, filling it with the chickpea mixture.
Fold the pastry over the filling and brush with a little water to seal.
Bake the pasty in a preheated oven at 375 degrees for 40 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Allow the pasty to cool for a few minutes before serving.
